fields.label: Psychology (General)
fields.description: Catch-all for psychology articles that do not fit clearly into any specific subfield, or that span multiple psychological areas without a clear primary focus.
exclusions: Use this as last resort when no single subfield dominates

fields.label: Cognitive Psychology
fields.description: Perception, attention, memory, learning, reasoning, language, problem-solving, JDM (heuristics, biases)

fields.label: Social Psychology
fields.description: Attitudes, persuasion, social influence, stereotypes, prejudice, group dynamics, social identity, social cognition, close relationships
exclusions: Workplace focus where org is main focus → Occupational Psychology

fields.label: Clinical Psychology, Diagnostics & Psychotherapy
fields.description: Psychopathology, diagnosis, assessment, psychotherapy, mental health treatment, counselling

fields.label: Developmental Psychology
fields.description: Development across lifespan, childhood/adolescence, cognitive/social/emotional/moral development

fields.label: Biological & Neuropsychology
fields.description: Brain imaging, psychophysiology, neuropsychological assessment, behavioural genetics, evolutionary psychology

fields.label: Educational Psychology
fields.description: Learning, instruction, motivation in educational settings, academic achievement, self-regulated learning

fields.label: Occupational, Organisational & Business Psychology
fields.description: Work/org-specific constructs: leadership, burnout, job satisfaction, team performance, HR practices, safety climate
exclusions: Workplace as convenient setting → classify by theory domain
Stereotype threat in interviews → Social Psychology

fields.label: Differential & Personality Psychology and Diagnostics
fields.description: Individual differences, personality traits, temperament, intelligence, psychological assessment

fields.label: Methods & Evaluation in Psychology
fields.description: Research methods, measurement models, psychometrics, program evaluation, statistical methods
exclusions: Methods in other discipline → that discipline
